frameworks.name: stochastic resonance
frameworks.one_line: added electrical noise improves weak-signal detection in sensorimotor cortex

frameworks.name: neuronal threshold modulation
frameworks.one_line: tRNS shifts firing threshold to change cortical excitability

frameworks.name: oscillation entrainment
frameworks.one_line: noise-driven engagement of endogenous cortical rhythms

core_question: How does transcranial random noise stimulation modulate sensory and motor cortical circuits, from cellular mechanisms up to behavior?
key_assumptions: noise at cellular and systems levels affects neural signal processing
exogenous electrical noise can be delivered non-invasively via transcranial stimulation
manipulations: addition of transcranial electrical noise to cortical circuits
application across sensory versus motor circuits
key_findings: tRNS produces measurable cellular and behavioral effects across sensory and motor circuits
tRNS can enhance function in both healthy individuals and neurologic patients
candidate mechanisms include stochastic resonance, threshold modulation, and oscillation entrainment
interpretations: tRNS is a promising but still poorly understood neuromodulation technique
behavioral enhancement can be explained by noise-based amplification of subthreshold signals
limitations_noted: tRNS mechanisms are less well characterized than tDCS
